steps.title: Recognize the conditional probability from the phrase
steps.content: The question asks for the fraction of chocolate-colored kittens — this phrase signals conditional probability. We need P(deep blue eyes | chocolate coat), not the overall proportion. The denominator is the total number of chocolate kittens, not all 48.

steps.title: Look up values and form the fraction
steps.content:
From the chocolate row: 12 have deep blue eyes out of 16 total chocolate kittens. The fraction is: $$\frac{12}{16}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
